Step 2: Option B claims step 2-3 doesn’t rely on external models. But the article explicitly uses ChatGPT and Wizard-E to generate instructions and assess correctness — external models are needed. So, B is false.

Step 3: Option C states that all WizardMath sizes exceed closed-source models. The article says WizardMath 70B surpasses GPT-4 and Claude-2, and that it outperforms others on GSM8k and MATH. It also says it surpasses ChatGPT, Claude, PaLM-2, etc. This is supported in the text.

Step 4: Option D claims the article ensures no harmful content. There's no mention of harmful content mitigation — only ethical implications are briefly noted. So D is not supported.

Best supported by evidence: C.
